stemHtml: Considering satellite meteorological data, the difference between visible and infrared (IR) images is that…

options.a: IR imagery is more reliable during summer when there is a greater temperature difference between the land and clouds.
options.b: a layer of cirrus clouds may be difficult to detect using visible imagery.
options.c: using IR imagery to identify how high clouds are is very difficult and unreliable.
options.d: low clouds may be difficult to identify on the IR image.
